brands.name: Crumbl Cookies
brands.note: Not currently selling new US territories. All US territories are sold out; ownership is only available via resale of existing stores (Franchise Empire, 2025, https://www.franchiseempire.com/articles/crumbl-cookie-franchise). Crumbl's own franchising page (https://crumblcookies.com/franchising) shows a US availability map without listing open markets.

FDD does not disclose specific territories.

brands.name: Daisy

nationwide.note: 'Franchises available throughout the United States.' Smart-home installation franchise; ~15 locations in 6 states (CA, FL, CT, AZ, MI/IN, NJ) with prime territories still open nationally.

brands.note: Backed by $15M Series C to accelerate franchise growth toward 100 locations (Franchise Times, 2025).

brands.name: Designery, The
brands.note: Actively franchising (Homefront Brands kitchen/bath design franchise; 117 territories awarded per Franchise Sidekick) but no public state-availability list found. Homefront Brands page only says 'Chat with us to understand our territory availability' (https://homefrontbrands.com/brands/the-designery/); Franchising.com listing shows it not currently accepting applicants there. Honest gap — no state-level evidence.

brands.name: Dunkin'

nationwide.note: Actively recruiting US franchisees nationally; 2025 development incentives ('Go West' ~$593K/unit, 'Brewed for Growth' ~$724K/unit, NRO incentive in select developing markets) target under-penetrated Western and Southern states. ~8,465 franchised US units.

brands.note: Mature Northeast markets are heavily built out, so new-unit growth is concentrated in developing/Western/Southern markets; existing-market entry often via resale.
